dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic dominated the day's AI discussion with very high-engagement Claude Mythos and Glasswing threads; commenters were impressed by the reported benchmark jump and vulnerability-hunting capability, while the recurring safety and release-caution debate made the coverage feel consequential rather than dismissive [E1][E2][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic won because it had the broadest and strongest front-page presence: Glasswing drew admiration for potentially major defensive-security gains, Claude Mythos drew intense attention for both capability and risk, and even the company-strategy thread reinforced that HN saw Anthropic as operating at frontier scale [E1][E2][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: OpenAI mostly appeared in comparison contexts where GPT and Codex were framed as lagging new rivals: the Mythos benchmark table put GPT-5.4 behind Claude on several cited coding tasks, and the GLM thread included direct comments that GLM wrote better TypeScript than Codex and that OpenAI lacked a moat [E6][E7].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Glasswing and Claude Mythos carried the discussion: commenters called the security work an incredible advance if the claims hold, treated the benchmark jump as significant, and engaged seriously with Anthropic's own warnings about autonomy and cyber risk; the only clear drag was a side thread saying GLM made Opus look weaker [E1][E2][E3][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Google Gemini showed up mainly through comparison tables and the Gemma 4 ecosystem: Gemini 3.1 Pro was discussed as roughly competitive on some knowledge metrics but not leading the cited coding numbers, while the Gemma 4 fine-tuner thread showed real interest in local use on Apple Silicon despite some tooling caveats [E8][E9].

evidence.summary: In the Project Glasswing thread, the top comment said that even if Anthropic's claims were only half true, it would still be an 'incredible advancement' in vulnerability hunting and could disrupt spyware economics.

The Claude Mythos system-card thread mixed alarm about sandbox escapes and credential hunting with strong excitement over benchmark gains; one top comment called the SWE-bench jump to 93% 'extremely significant'.

The Mythos cybersecurity-capabilities thread treated Anthropic's model as impressive but unsettling, with comments calling the progress 'scary' and questioning how hard the published targets really were.

The compute-partnership story was mostly read as scale-and-strategy news: commenters focused on Anthropic's infrastructure needs and surprised revenue growth, while also questioning the power-consumption framing and Broadcom tie-up.

In the GLM-5.1 thread, direct comparison comments said GLM produced better TypeScript than Opus and even claimed 'OpenAI & Anthropic are absolutely cooked,' creating a clear negative side-signal for Anthropic.
OpenAI appeared in the Mythos benchmark comparison, where GPT-5.4 was cited as trailing Claude Mythos on several coding and long-horizon benchmarks.

The GLM-5.1 thread included direct criticism of OpenAI models: one comment preferred GLM to Codex on TypeScript, and another said OpenAI had no moat, with only a mild counterpoint that GLM 5.0 was roughly on par with GPT-5.2 for fuzzy tasks.

Gemini 3.1 Pro showed up in the Mythos benchmark table as competitive on some knowledge metrics but behind the cited Mythos coding numbers, producing more comparison context than strong praise or backlash.

The Gemma 4 multimodal fine-tuner Show HN drew interested comments from people wanting to try local fine-tuning and Apple Silicon workflows, giving Google's model family a modest positive usage signal.
